titleEn: Robot Raggy taking on one of farming’s most persistent weed problems
titleZh: 机器人Raggy挑战农业中最顽固的杂草问题
summaryZh: 英国机器人公司Robotriks开发了一款名为Raggy的电动农业机器人，专门用于识别和机械清除对牲畜有毒的杂草——千里光（ragwort）。该机器人基于Robotic Traction Unit（RTU）平台，配备机器视觉和AI，可实时决策并根除杂草，成本仅为7000英镑，约为同类产品的十分之一。其模块化设计还支持精准喷洒、自主种植和作物监测等任务，对减少化学除草剂使用、保护土壤和动物健康具有重要价值。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了低成本、模块化农业机器人在杂草精准清除方面的创新应用，结合AI视觉和机械根除技术，对减少化学除草剂依赖、保护牲畜健康具有直接参考价值，适合关注自动化与可持续农业的从业者阅读。
contentFull: Robot Raggy taking on one of farming’s most persistent weed problems 10-06 | Field robots | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintFor the Raggy project, the RTU has been equipped with sophisticated detection and removal capabilities. Using machine vision and connected technology, it locates ragwort plants and mechanically removes them at the root. Photo: Dorset Council.A small autonomous robot is preparing to head out into the fields of Dorset this summer – and farmers dealing with ragwort infestations are watching closely. Raggy, an electrically powered farm robot developed by UK robotics firm Robotriks, has arrived at Dorset Innovation Park near Wareham for its final round of testing before commencing field trials across farms and land in the county. For farmers and landowners, the arrival could hardly come soon enough.Ragwort (Jacobaea vulgaris) is one of Britain’s most problematic agricultural weeds. It can contaminate grazing land and cause serious health problems if eaten by livestock. The plant contains alkaloids that cause irreversible liver damage. For arable and mixed farmers, it represents both a regulatory obligation and a significant drain on time and labour. Currently, control is usually targeted, with plants removed from areas where they pose a risk to livestock. The standard method – hand-pulling – is slow, costly, and physically demanding work that also carries risks to those doing it. How Raggy worksAt the heart of the project is Robotriks’ Robotic Traction Unit (RTU), a platform the company has been developing and refining since its early days at the University of Plymouth. The RTU costs just £7,000 (€8,114) – almost a tenth of the cost of most comparable products on the market. Powered by batteries lasting up to 24 hours, it can be deployed for a wide range of tasks from crop monitoring to harvesting. For the Raggy project, the RTU has been equipped with sophisticated detection and removal capabilities. Using machine vision and connected technology, it locates ragwort plants and mechanically removes them at the root. The system is powered by the Qualcomm Dragonwing platform – meaning the robot can make decisions in the field in real time. The robot reduces the need for harmful chemicals, supports healthier soils and protects animals. What makes the platform particularly interesting for the wider farming community — including arable growers — is its modular design. The RTU is a fully electric, modular platform designed to perform a range of agricultural tasks in real farm conditions.Robotriks has previously demonstrated attachments for precision spraying, autonomous planting, and crop monitoring. With cameras and custom-trained AI to recognise different types of weeds and crops, the system can target and directly spray weeds growing too close to main crops – a capability with clear potential for arable farming, where herbicide resistance and the cost of blanket chemical applications are growing concerns.A collaborative projectRaggy has been developed collaboratively by Robotriks, technology solutions company Telint, and network architecture specialists Neutral Networks, with backing from Dorset Council. Funding came from Qualcomm Incorporated through its Qualcomm for Good Initiative. Ben Timmons, Senior Director of Business Development at Qualcomm Technologies International, described Raggy as ‘a powerful demonstration of what’s possible with intelligent connected technologies’.Dorset Council’s Leader, Cllr Nick Ireland, welcomed the trials, noting that council rangers currently spend considerable hours each year removing ragwort by hand from the county’s country parks and nature reserves.The trials are taking place at Dorset Innovation Park, which is part of an emerging South West Global Autonomy Cluster. The cluster has recently been awarded up to £20 million (€23,2 million) of government funding to enhance the region’s reputation as one of the world’s best places to develop, test and deploy autonomous technologies. translationZh:
一台小型自主机器人正准备今年夏天进入多塞特郡的田野——而正在应对千里光杂草问题的农民们正密切关注着。Raggy是由英国机器人公司Robotriks开发的一款电动农业机器人，已抵达韦勒姆附近的多塞特创新园，进行最终测试，随后将在该郡的农场和土地上进行田间试验。对于农民和土地所有者来说，这款机器人的到来可谓恰逢其时。

千里光（Jacobaea vulgaris）是英国最具问题的农业杂草之一。它会污染牧场，如果被牲畜食用，会导致严重的健康问题。这种植物含有生物碱，会造成不可逆的肝脏损伤。对于大田作物和混合农场来说，它既是监管义务，也是时间和劳动力的巨大消耗。目前，控制措施通常是针对性的，将植物从对牲畜有风险的区域移除。标准方法——手工拔除——速度慢、成本高、劳动强度大，且对操作者也有风险。

**Raggy的工作原理**

该项目的核心是Robotriks的Robotic Traction Unit（RTU），这是该公司自普利茅斯大学早期以来一直在开发和改进的平台。RTU的成本仅为7000英镑（约8114欧元）——几乎是市场上同类产品价格的十分之一。它由可持续工作长达24小时的电池供电，可执行从作物监测到收获的多种任务。对于Raggy项目，RTU配备了先进的检测和清除能力。利用机器视觉和连接技术，它可以定位千里光植物，并机械地将其连根拔除。该系统由高通Dragonwing平台驱动——这意味着机器人可以在田间实时做出决策。该机器人减少了对有害化学品的需求，支持更健康的土壤，并保护动物。

对于更广泛的农业社区（包括大田作物种植者）来说，该平台特别有趣的地方在于其模块化设计。RTU是一个全电动、模块化的平台，旨在真实农场条件下执行多种农业任务。Robotriks此前已展示了用于精准喷洒、自主种植和作物监测的附件。通过摄像头和定制训练的AI来识别不同类型的杂草和作物，该系统可以瞄准并直接喷洒离主要作物太近的杂草——这一能力对大田作物种植具有明显潜力，因为除草剂抗性和全面化学喷洒的成本正成为日益增长的担忧。

**一个合作项目**

Raggy
author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: A small autonomous robot is preparing to head out into the fields of Dorset this summer – and farmers dealing with ragwort infestations are watching closely. Raggy, an electrically powered farm robot developed by UK robotics firm Robotriks, has arrived at Dorset Innovation Park near Wareham for its final round of testing before commencing field trials across farms and land in the county. For farmers and landowners, the arrival could hardly come soon enough.
